body:
MORNING INTEL — 2026-07-13 (snapshot 2026-07-13_1)
Regime data: VIX 16.25 | HY OAS 270 | Breadth 90.9%
AMZN: NO_TRADE — IV Rank below minimum or missing (G2_iv_rank: observed None threshold >= 25.0)
NVDA: NO_TRADE — IV Rank below minimum or missing (G2_iv_rank: observed None threshold >= 25.0)
SPY: NO_TRADE — IV Rank below minimum or missing (G2_iv_rank: observed None threshold >= 25.0)
Portfolio risk after fills: pending Artha confirmation
Discipline: 30d system PnL $0 | override PnL $0
Data quality: all sources OK

structured.takeaway: The current regime signals a cautious market environment with VIX at 16.25, showing mild near-term volatility but a contango structure suggesting some risk premium remains priced in, while HY OAS at 270 bps and breadth near 91% indicate moderate credit stress and decent market participation. SPY at $754.95 with neutral sentiment and low IV (7.88%) reflects indecision amid mixed macro signals, but NVDA’s $210.96 price and negative sentiment (-1) alongside 9.15% IV highlight growing bearish skepticism on AI investments, amplified by Michael Burry’s high-profile bets against the sector, which could pressure tech leadership. AMZN’s $245.34 with neutral sentiment and higher IV (9.82%) suggests some uncertainty but no clear directional bias absent fresh catalysts. The key near-term catalyst is the cluster of major bank earnings starting July 14, which will provide critical insight into credit conditions and risk appetite; any signs of weakness could exacerbate volatility and push SPY lower. A building risk is the unresolved Fed reform deadlock, which could prolong policy uncertainty and keep yields elevated (US10Y at 4.54%), pressuring growth stocks further. System is in warmup, so IV rank filters are not yet constraining signals.

SPY.sentiment_driver: Mixed sentiment on overall market outlook
SPY.gate_failure_detail: IV Rank below minimum or missing

NVDA.sentiment_driver: Concerns over AI investments and bearish bets
NVDA.gate_failure_detail: IV Rank below minimum or missing

AMZN.gate_failure_detail: IV Rank below minimum or missing

news.title: The Smartest Artificial Intelligence (AI) Investment Moves to Make With $5,000 in July
It's Been Over 7 Weeks, and Fed Chair Kevin Warsh Has Failed to Make Any Headway on a Key Central Bank Reform
Does "The Big Short's" Michael Burry Know Something Wall Street Doesn't? He Just Placed Bets Against AI and Sees the "Beginning of the End."

narratives.this_week: Bank earnings dominate this week with JPM, BAC, WFC, and GS reporting on July 14, followed by MS on July 15. Investors will focus on credit quality and trading revenue as key indicators of financial sector health amid current macro volatility (VIX 16.25, HY OAS 270 bps). Positive surprises could buoy SPY given financials’ weight, while muted guidance may pressure risk sentiment and weigh on cyclical names like GE and PLD also reporting midweek. Watch UNH and NFLX earnings on July 16 for health care and communication services signals, with UNH expected bullish and NFLX bearish, potentially influencing defensive versus growth positioning.
narratives.next_week: Next week features major tech and communication services earnings, notably GOOGL, TSLA, and T on July 22, with RTX and HON in industrials also in focus. GOOGL’s bullish outlook could support NVDA and broader tech, reinforcing AI and cloud growth narratives, while TSLA’s neutral stance tempers enthusiasm in consumer discretionary. Real estate and materials sectors report as well, providing a barometer for economic resilience; strong results here could underpin cyclical rotation in SPY, especially if industrials confirm steady demand.
narratives.next_month: Looking further ahead, the July 29–31 window is packed with heavyweight tech earnings from MSFT, META, AAPL, and AMZN, alongside the FOMC meeting and July PCE data. MSFT and AAPL’s bullish leanings contrast with META’s bearish outlook, setting up a critical test for tech leadership and growth stock valuations. The FOMC is widely expected to hold rates, but PCE inflation readings above 0.2% risk pressuring equities and favoring defensive sectors. This confluence of events will be pivotal for SPY’s trajectory and could influence positioning in high-beta names like NVDA and AMZN ahead of August’s labor market and CPI releases.
